page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 248 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core. The processor architecture is x86_64, incorporating a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It lacks local storage and bundled GPUs, and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific workloads. The instance achieves a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 30.35 GB/sec and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 187.69 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Geekbench testing shows a strong single-core score of 1933.0 and a strong text processing score of 2637.0. In terms of memory performance, the cached read-only small block bandwidth reaches 2186.0 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write bandwidths are 144.63 GB/sec and 61.79 GB/sec, respectively.

The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). These instructions contribute to strong performance in low-parameter machine learning tasks, as demonstrated by a top-tier text generation speed of 421.19 tokens/sec for a small 135M LLM. However, a significant performance tradeoff is observed with larger models; text generation for a 70B LLM drops to a poor rate of 2.38 tokens/sec, reflecting the limitations of CPU-only inference for massive models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-32 offers a dense allocation of memory relative to compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of higher vCPU tiers. It is well-suited for high-throughput static web serving, memory-intensive database operations, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance configured with 32 vCPUs and 248 GB of RAM. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 16 physical cores, it features a substantial 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results show strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression (30.35 GB/sec), multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (187.69 GB/sec), and small-model LLM text generation (421.19 tokens/sec). However, performance drops significantly for large-scale LLM inference, with 70B model text generation falling to 2.38 tokens/sec. This instance is highly suitable for memory-intensive database operations, static web serving, and moderate data processing workloads that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering strong performance for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 features 32 vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring 248 GB RAM and Intel Xeon processors, built for memory-intensive databases and data processing.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 248 GB RAM powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core for memory-bound applications.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Strong performance in static web serving and small-model LLM inference.
No local storage or dedicated GPU resources included.
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, data processing, and web serving.
